git_workflow_for_pr_updates.overview:
Critical instructions for properly updating an existing PR with your vulnerability remediation fixes. This ensures the CI/CD workflow triggers correctly and test results are properly tracked.
EXECUTION CONTEXTS: 1. Bob Shell (GitHub Actions): Branch name provided explicitly in workflow prompt 2. Bob IDE: Branch name extracted from PR metadata

critical_principle.title: ALWAYS Commit to the Existing PR Branch
critical_principle.rule: When fixing unit tests for an existing PR, you MUST commit your changes to the PR's existing source branch, NOT create a new branch.

why_this_matters.reason: The PR is already created and linked to a specific branch
Committing to that branch automatically updates the PR
This triggers the CI/CD workflow to re-run tests
The workflow can then assign reviewers based on test results
Creating a new branch would create a separate PR, not update the existing one

bob_shell_emphasis.critical:
In bob-shell context (GitHub Actions), the workflow prompt EXPLICITLY provides the branch name you must use. This takes absolute priority.
Example prompt: "Commit your fixes to the EXISTING PR branch: fix/cve-2023-12345-flask"
You MUST use this exact branch name in all git commands. Do NOT try to derive it from PR metadata if it's in the prompt.

identifying_pr_branch.title: Step 1: Identify the PR's Source Branch

priority.when: Prompt contains "Commit to branch" or "EXISTING PR branch:"
priority.action: Extract and use the exact branch name from the prompt
priority.example: Prompt: "Commit your fixes to the EXISTING PR branch: fix/cve-2023-12345-flask" → Use: fix/cve-2023-12345-flask
priority.critical: This is the authoritative source in bob-shell context. The workflow has already determined the correct branch. Use it exactly as provided - no parsing, no modification.

priority.when: Branch not explicitly provided in prompt

from_workflow_prompt.title: Extracting Branch from Workflow Prompt (Bob Shell)
from_workflow_prompt.description: The bob-shell-v2.yml workflow provides the branch name explicitly in the prompt to ensure you commit to the correct branch.

patterns.pattern: "Commit your fixes to the EXISTING PR branch: [branch-name]"

from_workflow_prompt.extraction_example:
Full prompt: "Fix the failing unit tests in PR #38.
Follow this workflow: 1. Analyze the PR at https://github.com/org/repo/pull/38 2. Understand what package was upgraded and why tests are failing 3. Run the unit tests locally to see the exact failures 4. Fix the code to work with the upgraded package 5. Verify all tests pass 6. Commit your fixes to the EXISTING PR branch: fix/cve-2023-12345-flask 7. Push the changes 8. Add a comment to the PR documenting what was fixed
CRITICAL: Commit to branch 'fix/cve-2023-12345-flask' - do NOT create a new branch!"
Extracted branch: fix/cve-2023-12345-flask
from_workflow_prompt.store: Store this branch name immediately when you see it in the prompt. You will use it in Step 2 for all git commands.

from_pr_metadata.title: Extracting Branch from PR Metadata (Bob IDE or Fallback)
from_pr_metadata.description: When you fetch the PR via GitHub MCP or fetch_github_issue, the response includes the branch information in the PR metadata.

look_for.field: The user who created the branch

from_pr_metadata.example:
PR #38 metadata shows: - head.ref: "fix/cve-2023-12345-flask" - base.ref: "main"
This means: - Source branch: fix/cve-2023-12345-flask - Target branch: main - You must commit to: fix/cve-2023-12345-flask

from_pr_description.description: PR descriptions typically show the branch merge information
from_pr_description.pattern: "[user] wants to merge [N] commit(s) into [base] from [head-branch]"
from_pr_description.example:
"highorbit25 wants to merge 1 commit into main from fix/cve-2023-12345-flask"
Extract: fix/cve-2023-12345-flask

store_branch_name.action: Remember this branch name for all subsequent git commands

git_workflow_steps.title: Step 2: Execute Git Commands to Update PR

step.name: checkout_pr_branch
step.action: Switch to the PR's source branch
step.critical:
This MUST be the exact branch name from Step 1.
In bob-shell context: Use the branch name from the workflow prompt In bob-ide context: Use the branch name from PR metadata (head.ref)
Do NOT create a new branch with git checkout -b. Do NOT use a different branch name. Do NOT modify or parse the branch name.

if_branch_not_exists.example: git fetch origin fix/cve-2023-12345-flask git checkout fix/cve-2023-12345-flask

step.name: verify_branch
step.action: Confirm you're on the correct branch
step.must_match: The PR's head branch name exactly

if_wrong_branch.fix: Go back to step 1 and checkout the correct branch

verify.check: Verify the correct files are staged

step.name: commit_changes
step.action: Commit with a clear, descriptive message
step.message_format:
fix: resolve unit test failures after [package] upgrade
- [Specific change 1] - [Specific change 2] - [Specific change 3]
Addresses failing tests in PR #[number] Related to CVE-[id]
step.example:
git commit -m "fix: resolve unit test failures after Flask upgrade
- Fixed import paths for Flask v3.1.0 - Updated test assertions to match new behavior - Replaced deprecated request.json with request.get_json()
Addresses failing tests in PR #38 Related to CVE-2024-12345"

step.name: push_to_pr_branch
step.action: Push to the PR's source branch
step.critical: The branch name MUST match the PR's head branch exactly. This is the most critical step - pushing to the correct branch updates the PR.

what_happens.immediate: - Your commit is added to the PR - PR is updated with your changes - PR shows "1 new commit" or similar
what_happens.automatic: - GitHub triggers the CI/CD workflow - .github/workflows/pr-unit-test.yml runs - Unit tests are executed - Based on results, reviewers are assigned: * If tests pass → Software Lead is requested for review * If tests fail → SWE is assigned to fix remaining issues

check.how: View the PR on GitHub and check the commits tab
check.expected: Your commit message appears in the PR's commit history

check.how: Check the PR's "Checks" tab or Actions tab
check.expected: pr-unit-test.yml workflow is running or completed

check.how: Wait for workflow to complete and check results
check.expected: - If tests pass: ✅ All checks passed - If tests fail: ❌ Tests failed (need more fixes)

mistake.name: creating_new_branch
mistake.description: Creating a new branch instead of using the PR's branch
mistake.why_wrong: This creates a completely new branch that's not connected to the PR. Pushing this would require creating a new PR, not updating the existing one.
mistake.correct_approach: Use git checkout [existing-pr-branch] to switch to the PR's branch

mistake.name: wrong_branch_name
mistake.description: Using a different branch name than the PR's head branch
mistake.wrong_example: PR branch: fix/cve-2023-12345-flask You push to: fix-tests or main
mistake.why_wrong: The PR is tracking a specific branch. Pushing to a different branch won't update the PR.
mistake.correct_approach: Always use the exact branch name from the PR metadata

mistake.name: pushing_to_main
mistake.why_wrong: - Bypasses the PR review process - Doesn't trigger the PR's CI/CD workflow - May be blocked by branch protection rules - Doesn't update the PR
mistake.correct_approach: Always push to the PR's source branch, never to main

mistake.name: not_fetching_branch
mistake.description: Trying to checkout a branch that doesn't exist locally
mistake.error: error: pathspec 'fix/cve-2023-12345-flask' did not match any file(s)
mistake.why_happens: The PR branch exists on the remote but not in your local repository

pr_documentation.title: Step 3: Document Your Fixes in the PR

why_document.reason: Reviewers need to understand what was fixed and why
Creates a permanent record of the remediation work
Explains the root cause of test failures
Shows that all tests now pass
Provides context for the Software Lead review

how_to_document.action: Add a comment to the PR using GitHub MCP

use_mcp_tool.arguments:
{ "method": "create", "owner": "your-org", "repo": "concert-bob-remediate", "pullNumber": 38, "body": "## Unit Test Fixes

I've fixed the failing unit tests caused by the Flask upgrade.

### Changes Made
- Updated import path from `flask.json` to `flask` in main.py
- Modified test assertions to use `request.get_json()` instead of `request.json`

### Root Cause
The upgrade from Flask v2.0.1 to v3.1.0 introduced these breaking changes:
- `flask.json` module was removed
- `request.json` property deprecated

### Testing
- All 3 unit tests now pass ✅
- No regressions introduced
- Security fix verified

### Files Modified
- `app/vulnerable/main.py`: Updated Flask imports
- `app/vulnerable/test_main.py`: Updated test assertions

Ready for review!", "event": "COMMENT" }

pr_documentation.comment_template:
## Unit Test Fixes
I've fixed the failing unit tests caused by the [package] upgrade.
### Changes Made - [Specific change 1 with file name] - [Specific change 2 with file name] - [Specific change 3 with file name]
### Root Cause The upgrade from v[old] to v[new] introduced these breaking changes: - [Breaking change 1] - [Breaking change 2]
### Testing - All [N] unit tests now pass ✅ - No regressions introduced - Security fix verified and maintained
### Files Modified - `[file1]`: [reason for change] - `[file2]`: [reason for change]
Ready for review!

formatting_tips.tip: Use backticks for file names and code
Be specific about what changed and why

check.how: View the PR on GitHub and check the commits tab
check.expected: Your commit message appears in the PR's commit history

check.what: Your comment appears on the PR
check.expected: Your documentation comment is visible with all the details

check.how: Check the PR's "Checks" tab or Actions tab
check.expected: pr-unit-test.yml workflow is running or completed

check.how: Wait for workflow to complete and check results
check.expected: - If tests pass: ✅ All checks passed - If tests fail: ❌ Tests failed (need more fixes)
- If tests pass: Software Lead requested for review - If tests fail: SWE assigned for more fixes

workflow_integration.title: How This Integrates with the CI/CD Workflow

trigger.explanation: The "synchronize" event is triggered when you push new commits to the PR branch. This is why pushing to the correct branch is critical.

workflow_steps.step: Checkout code from the PR branch
Based on results, assign reviewers: - All pass → Request review from Software Lead - Any fail → Assign to SWE for more fixes

workflow_integration.why_branch_matters:
When you push to the PR branch, GitHub: 1. Detects the push as a "synchronize" event on the PR 2. Triggers the pr-unit-test.yml workflow 3. Runs tests on your updated code 4. Updates the PR with test results 5. Assigns appropriate reviewers
If you push to a different branch, none of this happens.

quick_reference.title: Quick Reference: Correct Git Workflow

checklist.item: ✓ Identify PR branch (Priority 1: from workflow prompt, Priority 2: from PR metadata)
✓ Store the exact branch name (e.g., fix/cve-2023-12345-flask)
✓ Checkout that exact branch: git checkout [pr-branch]
✓ Verify you're on correct branch: git branch --show-current
✓ Commit with clear message: git commit -m "..."
✓ Push to PR branch: git push origin [pr-branch]
✓ Add documentation comment to PR using GitHub MCP
✓ Wait for CI/CD workflow to run
✓ Check reviewer assignment based on test results

quick_reference.command_sequence:
# 1. Identify branch from workflow prompt or PR metadata # Example from prompt: "Commit to branch 'fix/cve-2023-12345-flask'" # Example from metadata: head.ref = "fix/cve-2023-12345-flask"
# 2. Checkout the PR branch (use exact name from step 1) git checkout fix/cve-2023-12345-flask
# 3. Verify you're on the right branch git branch --show-current
# 4. Stage your fixes git add app/vulnerable/main.py
# 5. Commit with descriptive message git commit -m "fix: resolve unit test failures after Flask upgrade
- Fixed import paths for Flask v3.1.0 - Updated test assertions
Addresses failing tests in PR #38"
# 6. Push to the PR branch (NOT a new branch!) git push origin fix/cve-2023-12345-flask
